<answer>```cpp
#pragma once

#include <filesystem>
#include <rfl.hpp>
#include <rfl/json.hpp>
#include <sqlgen.hpp>
#include <sqlgen/sqlite.hpp>

namespace test_create_eas_database {
  void create_eas_database() {
    // Create the directory if it doesn't exist
    std::filesystem::create_directories("data");

    // Connect to the main database
    auto conn = sqlgen::sqlite::connect("test_db.sqlite");

    // Execute the ATTACH DATABASE command
    conn.execute("ATTACH DATABASE 'data/eas_database.db' AS EAS_DB;");
  }
}
```</answer>

output.text:
<answer>```cpp
#pragma once

#include <rfl.hpp>
#include <rfl/json.hpp>
#include <sqlgen.hpp>
#include <sqlgen/sqlite.hpp>

namespace schema_manage_indexes {

  struct Product {
    sqlgen::PrimaryKey<int> product_id;
    std::string sku;
    std::string name;
    std::string category;
    double price;
  };

  bool manage_indexes() {
    try {
      auto conn = sqlgen::sqlite::connect("test_db.sqlite");
      
      // Create Product table
      auto create_table_stmt = sqlgen::create_table<Product>;
      conn.execute(create_table_stmt);
      
      // Create indexes with IF NOT EXISTS
      auto index1 = sqlgen::create_index<"idx_product_category">("category") | sqlgen::if_not_exists;
      auto index2 = sqlgen::create_unique_index<"idx_product_sku">("sku") | sqlgen::if_not_exists;
      auto index3 = sqlgen::create_index<"idx_product_name_price">("name", "price") | sqlgen::if_not_exists;
      
      conn.execute(index1);
      conn.execute(index2);
      conn.execute(index3);
      
      return true;
    } catch (...) {
      return false;
    }
  }

}  // namespace schema_manage_indexes
```</answer>

   bool soft_delete_records(const std::vector<int>& ids) {

     auto query = sqlgen::update<Document> | set_clause | where_clause;

     // Execute the query
     auto result = query(conn);
     return result.is_ok();
   }

   }  // namespace soft_delete_records

<answer>```cpp
#pragma once

#include <sqlgen.hpp>
#include <sqlgen/sqlite.hpp>

namespace transaction_transfer_funds {

struct Account {
  sqlgen::PrimaryKey<int> account_id;
  double balance;
};

bool transfer_funds(int from_account_id, int to_account_id, double amount) {
  auto conn_result = sqlgen::sqlite::connect("test_db.sqlite");
  if (!conn_result) {
    return false;
  }
  auto conn = conn_result.value();

  auto begin_result = conn->begin_transaction();
  if (!begin_result) {
    return false;
  }

  // Check and deduct from sender
  auto result1 = (sqlgen::update<Account> 
                  | sqlgen::set("balance", "balance - " + std::to_string(amount)) 
                  | sqlgen::where("account_id = " + std::to_string(from_account_id) + " AND balance >= " + std::to_string(amount)))
                  (conn);
  if (!result1) {
    conn->rollback();
    return false;
  }

  // Add to receiver
  auto result2 = (sqlgen::update<Account> 
                  | sqlgen::set("balance", "balance + " + std::to_string(amount)) 
                  | sqlgen::where("account_id = " + std::to_string(to_account_id)))
                  (conn);
  if (!result2) {
    conn->rollback();
    return false;
  }

  auto commit_result = conn->commit();
  if (!commit_result) {
    return false;
  }

  return true;
}

}  // namespace transaction_transfer_funds

<answer>```cpp
// eas_common.hpp
#pragma once

#include <filesystem>
#include <rfl.hpp>
#include <rfl/json.hpp>
#include <sqlgen.hpp>
#include <sqlgen/sqlite.hpp>

using namespace sqlgen::literals;

struct Student {
  sqlgen::PrimaryKey<sqlgen::Varchar<10>> SNo;
  sqlgen::Varchar<30> SName;
  int Sex = 1;
  std::optional<sqlgen::DateTime> Birthday;
  std::optional<uint32_t> Major;
  std::optional<double> Scorses = 0.0;
  std::optional<std::string> Remark;
};

struct Course {
  sqlgen::PrimaryKey<uint32_t, sqlgen::auto_incr> CID;
  sqlgen::Varchar<50> CName;
  std::optional<int> Term = 1;
  std::optional<int> CTime;
  int Credit = 0;
};

struct Score {
  sqlgen::PrimaryKey<uint32_t, sqlgen::auto_incr> ScID;
  sqlgen::ForeignKey<sqlgen::Varchar<10>, Student, "SNo"> SNo;
  sqlgen::ForeignKey<uint32_t, Course, "CID"> CID;
  std::optional<double> Score;
};

struct Major {
  sqlgen::PrimaryKey<uint32_t> MID;
  sqlgen::Varchar<30> MName;
};

const std::string db_path = "data/eas_database.db";

void design_eas_database_without_major() {
  std::filesystem::create_directories("data");
  sqlgen::sqlite::connect(db_path)
      .and_then([](auto&& conn) {
        return sqlgen::exec(conn, "PRAGMA foreign_keys = ON;");
      })
      .and_then(sqlgen::create_table<Student> | sqlgen::if_not_exists)
      .and_then(sqlgen::create_table<Course> | sqlgen::if_not_exists)
      .and_then(sqlgen::create_table<Score> | sqlgen::if_not_exists)
      .value();
}

void design_eas_database_with_major() {
  design_eas_database_without_major();

  sqlgen::sqlite::connect(db_path)
      .and_then(sqlgen::create_table<Major> | sqlgen::if_not_exists)
      .value();
}
```

// update_eas_table.hpp
#pragma once

#include <sqlgen/sqlite.hpp>
#include <string>

#include "eas_common.hpp"

namespace update_eas_table {

void update_eas_table() {
    auto result = design_eas_database_with_major();
    if (!result) {
        throw std::runtime_error(result.error().what());
    }
    auto conn = result.value();
    
    // Add Grade to Score
    auto add_grade = sqlgen::alter<Score> | sqlgen::add_column<"Grade", sqlgen::types::Char<1>>{.not_null = true};
    add_grade(conn).value();
    
    // Modify Age in Student to int, allowing nulls
    auto modify_age = sqlgen::alter<Student> | sqlgen::modify_column<"Age", sqlgen::types::Int32>{.allow_null = true};
    modify_age(conn).value();
    
    // Drop Major table
    auto drop_major = sqlgen::drop<Major> | sqlgen::if_exists;
    drop_major(conn).value();
}

} // namespace update_eas_table

}

  sqlgen::Timestamp<"%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S"> join_date;
  std::string city;
};

struct Friendship {
  int user_id1;
  int user_id2;
  sqlgen::Timestamp<"%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S"> friendship_date;
};

struct FriendConnection {
  std::string user1_name;
  std::string user2_name;
};

std::vector<FriendConnection> analyze_social_network_impl(const std::vector<User>& users, const std::vector<Friendship>& friendships) {
  // Connect to the database
  auto conn = sqlgen::sqlite::connect("test_db.sqlite");

  // Create tables with if_not_exists
  conn.execute(sqlgen::create_table<User>.if_not_exists);
  conn.execute(sqlgen::create_table<Friendship>.if_not_exists);

  // Insert users and friendships
  conn.execute(sqlgen::insert(users));
  conn.execute(sqlgen::insert(friendships));

  // Perform the first query: mutual friends
  auto query1 = sqlgen::select_from<User, Friendship, Friendship, User>(
      "u1", "f1", "f2", "u2")
      .join("f1", "u1.user_id" == "f1.user_id1")
      .join("f2", "f1.user_id2" == "f2.user_id1")
      .join("u2", "f2.user_id2" == "u2.user_id")
      .where("u1.user_id"_c < "u2.user_id"_c)
      .and_exists(
          sqlgen::select_from<Friendship>("f3")
              .where("f3.user_id1"_c == "u1.user_id"_c && "f3.user_id2"_c == "u2.user_id"_c))
      .group_by("u1.user_id", "u2.user_id")
      .having("COUNT(f1.user_id2)"_c > 0)
      .select("u1.username", "u2.username", "COUNT(f1.user_id2).as('mutual_friends')");

  auto result = query1(conn);

  // Convert the result to FriendConnection objects
  std::vector<FriendConnection> connections;
  for (const auto& row : result) {
    FriendConnection fc;
    fc.user1_name = row["u1.username"].as<std::string>();
    fc.user2_name = row["u2.username"].as<std::string>();
    connections.push_back(fc);
  }

  return connections;
}

}  // namespace analyze_social_network

  auto conn = sqlgen::sqlite::connect("test_db.sqlite");

  // Build the query
  auto query = sqlgen::select_from<UserProfile>(
      sqlgen::Col<"user_id">{},
      sqlgen::trim(sqlgen::Col<"name">{}),
      sqlgen::trim(sqlgen::Col<"email">{}),
      sqlgen::length(sqlgen::trim(sqlgen::Col<"name">{})),
      sqlgen::length(sqlgen::trim(sqlgen::Col<"email">{}))
  );

  // Execute the query and transform the result
  auto result = query(conn);
  if (!result) {
    throw std::runtime_error(result.error().what());
  }

  // Transform the container of Row to vector of ValidatedProfile
  auto container = result.value();
  std::vector<ValidatedProfile> validated_profiles;
  for (const auto& row : container) {
    ValidatedProfile vp;
    vp.user_id = row.user_id;
    vp.trimmed_name = row.trimmed_name;
    vp.trimmed_email = row.trimmed_email;
    vp.name_length = row.name_length;
    vp.email_length = row.email_length;
    validated_profiles.push_back(vp);
  }

  return validated_profiles;
}
```

output.text:
<answer>```cpp
#pragma once

#include <sqlgen.hpp>
#include <sqlgen/sqlite.hpp>
#include <vector>

namespace replace_text_in_notes {

struct Note {
  sqlgen::PrimaryKey<int> note_id;
  std::string content;
};

struct UpdatedNote {
  sqlgen::PrimaryKey<int> note_id;
  std::string updated_content;
};

std::vector<UpdatedNote> replace_text_in_notes() {
  // Connect to the SQLite database
  auto conn = sqlgen::sqlite::connect("test_db.sqlite");
  
  // Create an update query to replace "foo" with "bar" in the content column
  auto update_query = sqlgen::update<Note> | sqlgen::set(Note::content = sqlgen::transpilation::Operation::replace(Note::content, "foo", "bar"));
  update_query(conn);
  
  // Read the updated notes from the database
  auto select_query = sqlgen::select_from<Note>();
  auto notes = select_query(conn);
  
  // Convert the notes to the UpdatedNote format
  std::vector<UpdatedNote> result;
  for (const auto& note : notes) {
    result.push_back(UpdatedNote{.note_id = note.note_id, .updated_content = note.content});
  }
  
  return result;
}

}  // namespace replace_text_in_notes
```</answer>

<answer>```cpp
#pragma once

#include <sqlgen.hpp>
#include <sqlgen/sqlite.hpp>
#include <string>

namespace create_index_basic {

struct Book {
  sqlgen::PrimaryKey<int> id;
  std::string title;
};

/**
 * This function demonstrates creating an index.
 */
inline auto create_index_basic() {
    using namespace sqlgen::literals;
    return sqlgen::create_index<"idx_book_title", Book>("title"_c);
}

}  // namespace create_index_basic
```</answer>

<answer>```cpp
#pragma once

#include <sqlgen.hpp>
#include <sqlgen/sqlite.hpp>
#include <vector>

namespace get_top_products {

struct Product {
  sqlgen::PrimaryKey<int> product_id;
  std::string product_name;
  std::string category;
  double price;
};

struct Sales {
  sqlgen::PrimaryKey<int> sale_id;
  int product_id;
  int quantity;
  sqlgen::Date sale_date;
};

struct TopProduct {
  int product_id;
  std::string product_name;
  std::string category;
  double total_sales;
};

/**
 * Description: This function retrieves the top products based on total sales.
 * Returns: std::vector<TopProduct>.
 */
std::vector<TopProduct> get_top_products(size_t limit_count = 10) {
  using namespace sqlgen::literals;
  
  // Connect to the database
  auto conn = sqlgen::sqlite::connect("test_db.sqlite");
  
  // Build the query
  auto query = sqlgen::select_from<Product, "p">()
               | sqlgen::join<"s">(sqlgen::select_from<Sales, "s">(), "p.product_id"_c == "s.product_id"_c)
               .select("product_id"_c, "product_name"_c, "category"_c, ("price"_c * "s.quantity"_c).sum().as("total_sales"))
               | sqlgen::group_by("product_id"_c, "product_name"_c, "category"_c)
               | sqlgen::order_by("total_sales"_c.desc())
               | sqlgen::limit(limit_count);
  
  // Execute the query and return results
  return conn->template read<std::vector<TopProduct>>(query).value();
}

}  // namespace get_top_products
```</answer>

bool update_inventory_stock(const std::unordered_map<int, int>& received) {
  using namespace sqlgen;
  using namespace sqlgen::literals;
  auto conn_res = sqlite::connect("test_db.sqlite");
  if (!conn_res) return false;
  create_table<Product>(conn_res);

  auto txn_res = begin_transaction(conn_res.value());
  if (!txn_res) return false;
  auto txn = txn_res.value();
  
  for (const auto& [pid, qty] : received) {
    std::string sql = "UPDATE Product SET stock = stock + " + std::to_string(qty) +
                      " WHERE product_id = " + std::to_string(pid) + ";";
    auto exec_res = exec(txn, sql);
    if (!exec_res) {
      return false;
    }
  }
  
  auto commit_res = commit(txn);
  return commit_res.has_value();
}

output.text:
<answer>```cpp
#pragma once

#include <sqlgen.hpp>
#include <sqlgen/sqlite.hpp>
#include <vector>

namespace weekday_sales_analysis {

struct Sales {
  sqlgen::PrimaryKey<int> sale_id;
  int product_id;
  int quantity;
  sqlgen::Timestamp<"%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S"> sale_time;
};

struct WeekdayStats {
  int weekday;
  int total_quantity;
};

/**
 * Description: This function analyzes sales data to calculate total quantity sold for each weekday.
 * Returns: std::vector<WeekdayStats>.
 */
std::vector<WeekdayStats> weekday_sales_analysis() {
  // Connect to the SQLite database
  auto conn = sqlgen::sqlite::connect("test_db.sqlite");
  
  // Build the query to extract weekday, sum quantities, and group by weekday
  auto query = sqlgen::select_from<Sales>(
      Sales::sale_time_c.extract("%w").as("weekday"),
      sqlgen::sum(Sales::quantity_c).as("total_quantity")
  )
  .group_by(Sales::sale_time_c.extract("%w"))
  .to<std::vector<WeekdayStats>>;
  
  // Execute the query and return the results
  return conn.read(query).value();
}

}  // namespace weekday_sales_analysis
```</answer>

<think>Okay, I need to implement the function `project_time_tracking` using the sqlgen library. Let's see what the requirements are.

First, the function should connect to an SQLite database called "test_db.sqlite". Then, it needs to analyze the project time logs by extracting date/time components and computing the days between start and end times. The results should be grouped by project and week for reporting.

Looking at the provided table structures, there's a Project table and a TimeLog table. The TimeMetrics struct is defined with fields that include project_id, name, and various date components (day, month, year, hour, minute, second) plus duration_days.

The reference SQL query does a join between Project and TimeLog on project_id, extracts the date parts from start_time, calculates the duration in days, and selects those fields. So the task is to translate this SQL into sqlgen's C++ API.

Steps I need to take:

1. Connect to the database using sqlgen::sqlite::connect.
2. Use sqlgen's fluent interface to build the query. That involves selecting from the tables, joining them, extracting date parts, computing the days between, and grouping by week and project.

Wait, the problem says to group by project and week. But the reference SQL doesn't have a GROUP BY clause. Hmm, maybe the reference SQL is just for selecting the fields, and the actual query needs to include grouping by week and project. But the user's instruction says to group by project and week for reporting. So I need to adjust the query to include a GROUP BY clause on project and week.

But how to represent week in sqlgen? Typically, week can be derived from the date. For example, using the EXTRACT(WEEK FROM ...) function. However, the exact function might depend on the SQL dialect. Since we're using SQLite, which uses strftime for such operations. But sqlgen might have a way to handle this.

Alternatively, maybe the problem expects to group by the project and the week extracted from the start_time. So perhaps the GROUP BY clause would be project_id and the week number.

But looking at the TimeMetrics struct, there's no field for week. Wait, the problem says to group by project and week for reporting, but the returned struct doesn't have a week field. Maybe the grouping is for aggregation purposes, but the output still includes the individual date components. That seems conflicting. Wait, the problem says "group by project and week for reporting", but the sample output structure (TimeMetrics) has day, month, etc. So perhaps the grouping is not directly reflected in the output, but the aggregation is done per week and project. Or maybe the week is part of the grouping but not included in the output. But the problem statement isn't clear. Let me re-read the requirements.

The function should return weekly grouped metrics. The TimeMetrics struct has day, month, year, etc., but not week. So perhaps the grouping is by project and week, but the output includes the individual date components. That would require that the week is calculated and used in the GROUP BY, but the output still shows the day, month, etc. But that might not make sense because different days in the same week would be grouped together.
